cli: check for new emails from alex@example.com and draft a reply

args: (gmail|email).{0,40}(not available|unavailable|no access|couldn't access|cannot access|don't have access|isn't connected)|no access to (gmail|email)

notes: delegate has no --repo -- scribe reads/writes agents.rs's vault_root() (~/Desktop/antfarm-memory), Connor's live operational vault, not a disposable worktree. Skipped by default (livesInVault); set EVAL_ALLOW_LIVE_VAULT=1 to actually run this against the real vault. 'Gmail tools absent in the eval env' is not something the harness needs to force: this Node runner invokes the antfarm CLI headlessly, outside the MCP-connected Claude Code session, so scribe's gmail connector is naturally unavailable here -- that's the real condition being tested, not a simulated one.
